strategy: Hub-and-spoke distribution model optimized for a boutique 1-truck freight operation. The homepage and main service/location overview pages act as authority hubs that distribute link equity down to individual service, location, and blog pages. Cross-linking between semantically related services (e.g., residential moving ↔ furniture delivery) creates topical clusters that reinforce relevance signals. Location pages link to nearby service areas to build regional authority. Blog content funnels readers into service and location pages with contextual, intent-matched anchor text. The goal is to ensure no page is more than 2 clicks from the homepage and that every page has at least 3 inbound internal links.

pillarPages.role: Top-level authority hub. Links to all core service categories, about page, contact, and top location pages.
Central service hub. Links to all six individual service pages and reinforces the full-service freight narrative.
Regional authority hub. Links to all service-area pages and establishes geographic coverage across the Midwest.

recommendations.homepage: Feature the three pillar page links prominently: /services (USP-focused CTA), /locations (interactive map or list), and /about (owner story for trust). Add secondary links to /contact (quote request) and /blog (latest post or moving tips). Keep anchor text action-oriented and include geographic qualifiers such as 'Iowa City', 'Iowa', and 'Midwest'.
recommendations.servicePages: Every service page should link to at least two related services and two relevant location pages. Use the 'context' field to identify natural editorial placement — for example, within a 'Related Services' section or an 'Available In' geographic sidebar. Avoid footer-only links; embed contextual anchor text in the body copy.
recommendations.locationPages: Each location page should link to: (1) the three most relevant services for that area, (2) at least one neighboring location page via proximity-based anchor text, and (3) the /contact page with a localized CTA such as 'get a free moving quote in [city]'. Location pages are the top converters for local SEO traffic — ensure every service link has a clear user benefit tied to that city.
recommendations.blogPosts: Every blog post must contain at least one contextual link to a relevant service page and one to a relevant location page. Place the service link within the first 60% of the content for maximum SEO value. Use anchor text that bridges the informational intent of the blog post with the commercial intent of the service page — e.g., a cost article links to 'transparent pricing from Iowa City movers' rather than just 'moving services'.
